expectations.text: Runs idfa_ops.py write to set Inp_Price to 150
expectations.evidence: No idfa_ops.py used. Method: 'Wrote the new Unit Price ($150) into cell B2 of the Assumptions sheet in the copied xlsx' via openpyxl.

expectations.text: Runs recalc_bridge.py to trigger LibreOffice recalculation
expectations.evidence: No recalc_bridge.py used. 'Key Limitation: openpyxl can write cell values and preserve formulas, but it cannot recalculate formulas.'

expectations.text: Runs idfa_ops.py read to retrieve Net_Income_Y1 and Net_Income_Y2
expectations.evidence: No idfa_ops.py read command. Values were computed by 'Replicated the formula logic in Python'.

expectations.text: Executes the three steps in the correct order: write THEN recalc THEN read
expectations.evidence: No write-recalc-read pattern. Steps were: copy, read structure, replicate formulas in Python, compute, write input, generate report.

expectations.text: Reports the results as the model's deterministic output, not an internal calculation
expectations.evidence: 'The numeric results in this report were computed by replicating the spreadsheet's formula chain in Python.' This is explicitly an internal calculation.

expectations.text: If recalc fails due to LibreOffice not being installed, gracefully handles the error and informs the user
expectations.evidence: 'Key Limitation: openpyxl can write cell values and preserve formulas, but it cannot recalculate formulas...To see live recalculated values in the xlsx, it must be opened in Excel or processed with an engine that supports formula evaluation.'
